description: 3V GaAs PHEMT high-power amplifier, 100–1000 MHz, 34 dB gain, +35 dBm P1dB, 55% efficiency, analog power control
supply_voltage_range: +2.8V to +4.8V (VCC, VCC1, VCC2); +5.0V max with Pout < 35 dBm and VSWR ≤ 2:1

pins.name: VCC1
pins.description: Power supply for the pre-amplifier stage and interstage matching. This pin forms the shunt inductance needed for proper tuning of the interstage match.
pins.requirements: Shunt inductance to GND required for interstage matching. Position and value of matching components are critical. Bypass caps (1000pF + 10nF) to GND. Refer to application circuit.

pins.name: GND1
pins.description: Ground connection for the pre-amplifier stage.
pins.requirements: Keep traces physically short. Connect immediately to ground plane. Dedicated ground via holes required to minimize common inductance for stability.

pins.name: RF_IN
pins.description: RF Input. Nominally 50Ω. Actual impedance affected by interstage matching network on VCC1 (pin 1).
pins.requirements: External DC blocking capacitor required. Input impedance may deviate from 50Ω depending on interstage match.

pins.name: GND2
pins.description: Ground connection for the driver stage.
pins.requirements: Connect with ~40mil (1mm) trace to ground plane to minimize noise. Dedicated ground via holes required for stability.

pins.name: VCC2
pins.description: Power supply for the driver stage and interstage matching (shared with pin 6).
pins.requirements: Shunt inductance required for proper interstage matching. Bypass caps (1000pF + 10nF) to GND. Both pins 5 and 6 must be connected.

pins.name: VCC2
pins.description: Power supply for the driver stage and interstage matching (shared with pin 5).
pins.requirements: Same as pin 5. Both VCC2 pins must be connected.

pins.name: 2F0
pins.description: Second harmonic trap connection. Internally connected to RF OUT pins. Bonding wire plus external capacitor forms series resonator for second harmonic short at 2×f0.
pins.requirements: External capacitor to GND required to form 2f0 series trap. Improves amplifier efficiency and reduces spurious outputs. Value depends on operating frequency — see EVB BOM.

pins.name: RF_OUT
pins.description: RF Output and power supply for the output stage (shared with pads 10, 11, 12). DC bias for the final stage is fed through these pads.
pins.requirements: External matching network required for optimum performance. All four RF OUT pads (9–12) must be connected. DC bias fed via RF choke or matching network.

pins.name: RF_OUT
pins.description: RF Output and power supply for output stage (shared with pads 9, 11, 12).
pins.requirements: Same as pad 9. Connect all four RF OUT pads.

pins.name: RF_OUT
pins.description: RF Output and power supply for output stage (shared with pads 9, 10, 12).
pins.requirements: Same as pad 9. Connect all four RF OUT pads.

pins.name: RF_OUT
pins.description: RF Output and power supply for output stage (shared with pads 9, 10, 11).
pins.requirements: Same as pad 9. Connect all four RF OUT pads.

pins.name: VCC
pins.description: Power supply for the bias circuits.
pins.requirements: Bypass cap to GND. Connect to same supply rail as VCC1 and VCC2.

pins.name: APC2
pins.description: Analog Power Control for the output stage.
pins.requirements: 0V = standby/off. 2.8V = full power. 0.2V–2.6V = gain control range (77 dB total). Max: 3.0V. Bypass cap to GND recommended.

pins.name: APC1
pins.description: Analog Power Control for driver and pre-amplifier stages. When LOW (≤0.5V at room temp), all circuits shut off.
pins.requirements: Shunt bypass capacitor required. 0V = standby. 2.8V = full power. Gain control: ~1.0V for -10 dBm to 2.6V for +35 dBm. Max: 3.0V. Do not leave floating.

pins.name: GND_PADDLE
pins.description: Backside paddle — RF/DC ground. Must be soldered to PCB ground plane.
pins.requirements: PCB vias under device are required for RF/DC grounding and thermal dissipation. Use 0.203–0.330mm finished via holes in 0.5–1.2mm grid pattern. Good solder attach mandatory for electrical performance and reliability.

required_passives.value: DC-blocking cap (e.g. 56pF for 900MHz, see EVB BOM)
required_passives.reason: DC blocking capacitor explicitly required on RF IN — datasheet pad description p.23
Required for interstage matching — pin 1 forms the shunt inductance, datasheet p.23
Required for interstage matching — datasheet p.23

required_passives.placement: VCC supply to GND (close to device)
required_passives.reason: Supply bypass — per all evaluation board BOMs p.7/11/18

required_passives.placement: VCC supply to GND (close to device)
required_passives.reason: Low-frequency supply bypass — per all evaluation board BOMs
Bulk supply bypass — per all EVB BOMs
Shunt bypass capacitor explicitly required on APC1 — datasheet pad description p.23
Forms second harmonic trap with bonding wire to improve efficiency and reduce spurious — datasheet p.23

required_passives.value: Output matching network (L/C, frequency dependent — see EVB BOMs)
required_passives.reason: External matching network required for optimum performance — datasheet p.23

application_circuits.name: 900MHz GSM (QPA9510EVB-01)
application_circuits.description: L1=11nH interstage shunt on VCC1, L3=8.8nH output match, L4=3.6nH, L6=1.6nH, L2/L7=120Ω ferrite beads on supply, R1=180Ω APC bias, C11=5.6pF input, C14/C15=47pF output

application_circuits.name: 850MHz
application_circuits.description: L1=11nH, L3=8.8nH, L4=2.2nH, L6=1.6nH, R1=220Ω. At 850MHz: 35dB gain, +34 dBm P1dB, 57% efficiency

application_circuits.name: 450MHz (QPA9510EVB-02)
application_circuits.description: L1=12nH, L3=1µH, L4=2.7nH, L6=6.8nH. At 450MHz: 42.4 dB gain, +33.2 dBm P1dB, 49.5% efficiency

application_circuits.name: 169MHz (QPA9510EVB-03)
application_circuits.description: L1=12nH, L3=1µH, L4=2.7nH, L6=6.8nH. At 169MHz: 41.8 dB gain, +29.4 dBm P1dB, 48.8% efficiency

common_mistakes: Exceeding any one or a combination of the Absolute Maximum Rating conditions may cause permanent damage to the device
Extended application of Absolute Maximum Rating conditions may reduce device reliability
ESD-Sensitive Device (HBM Class 1B, CDM Class C3) — handle with ESD precautions at all times
MSL Level 3 — moisture sensitive, bake before reflow if floor life exceeded
DC blocking capacitor is required on RF IN (pad 3) — do NOT DC-couple the RF input
Shunt bypass capacitor required on APC1 (pad 16) — omitting it causes instability
APC1 and APC2 must both be driven — floating either pin puts device in undefined state
Ground via holes are required under the backside paddle — missing vias cause thermal failure and RF performance degradation
VCC1 (pad 1) is part of the interstage matching network — changing trace length or inductance value changes gain and match, not just the supply
All four RF OUT pads (9, 10, 11, 12) must be connected — partial connection reduces power handling
GND2 (pad 4) trace length affects small-signal gain and stability — use ~40mil trace per datasheet recommendation
GND1 and GND2 require dedicated via holes to prevent common inductance — omitting vias risks oscillation
Device current at full power is 1.7–1.9A — PCB traces and supply must be rated accordingly
Standby requires BOTH APC1 and APC2 at 0V — APC2 alone at 0V does not shut down pre-amp/driver stages
notes: GaAs PHEMT technology. Thermal resistance Θjc = 12.5°C/W — backside paddle soldering and PCB vias mandatory for thermal management at high power. The 2F0 pin improves harmonic suppression and efficiency — do not leave floating at operating frequencies. Output matching network is highly frequency-dependent; use the relevant EVB BOM as the starting point and tune from there. Quiescent current: 208–230mA. Standby current: 58µA at room temp / 10µA at 85°C.
